hawkish_phrases: a view was put forward that conditions were increasingly falling into place that would allow the intensity of monetary policy accommodation to be adapted
the Governing Council needed to regain more policy space and flexibility to adjust policy
there was broad agreement to emphasise...the need for monetary policy to remain persistent and patient
any reassessment of the monetary policy stance should proceed in a very gradual and cautious manner
a very substantial degree of monetary accommodation was still needed for inflation pressures to build up
dovish_phrases: inflation dynamics remained subdued and signs of progress towards a sustained adjustment in the path of inflation were generally judged to still be insufficient
patience, persistence and prudence were still needed, as this process was expected to take time
the recent volatility in the exchange rate represented a source of uncertainty, requiring close monitoring
Discomfort was widely expressed about the very prolonged period over which inflation had been – and was still expected to remain – distant from the Governing Council's aim
measures of underlying inflation...had yet to show convincing signs of a sustained upward trend
summary: The Governing Council held rates unchanged at 0.00% (main refinancing), confirmed APP purchases at €60 billion monthly until end-December 2017, with unanimous decision but dovish guidance emphasizing patience and persistence due to subdued inflation dynamics despite solid growth, while signaling autumn recalibration and monitoring euro volatility as a source of uncertainty.
